text: The patient was intubated with a double lumen endotracheal tube placed in right lateral decubitus and the left lung was deflated.
A large volume hemothorax was visualized upon entry into the pleural space.
Initially a five millimeter suction irrigator was used, but then upsized to a 10 millimeter to effectively remove 3.5 liters of blood and clot.
The thoracic cavity was carefully inspected.
It wasn't until all of the blood was evacuated, you can see that arrow, that a pulsatile bleed from an intercostal artery was visualized on the posterior wall.
The artery was coagulated proximally and distally using a ligature and monopolar cautery.
After copiously irrigating the thoracic cavity, the injured site was re-examined and confirmed for no active bleed.
A 32 French chest tube was then placed and the lung was re-inflated.
